body:
stalled: no write activity after 30 consecutive read-only tool calls
result_rev=ee9b397f84dfab2865c21b213e540746c6393236
base_rev=ee9b397f84dfab2865c21b213e540746c6393236
retry_after=2026-04-15T04:55:59Z

This is a distinct failure mode from the long-context hang seen on Stage 2.2. Here the model is actively producing tool calls (reading files) but never commits to a write/edit, so the stall detector after 30 reads cancels the run. Symptom of context-window exhaustion: the model is re-reading the same files or exploring too broadly without ever reaching a decision. Not a 500. Not a hang. Not a loop abort.

Escalated to claude sonnet per user directive. Local model is not viable for code-heavy beads with the current prompt shape (plan doc + 1908-line schema + bead context). Future cost-tiered routing epic should capture: prompt-context pre-filtering for local models, AND a check for "N consecutive reads without a write" that kicks in earlier than the 30-read cap.
summary: qwen/qwen3-coder-next stalled: 30 consecutive read-only tool calls with no write activity — stall detector triggered

body:
Opus fresh-eyes review of 45ef7ca.

IMPLEMENTATION: REAL. documents resolver uses Relay cursor connection (edges/pageInfo/totalCount), reads from docgraph.BuildGraphWithConfig. docGraph resolver returns real nodes+edges with pathToId/dependents as JSON-string per schema. TestGraphQLDocuments and TestGraphQLDocGraph both PASS with real server + real docgraph backend.

SHAPE COMPLIANCE vs Stage 2.3 note:
1. StateProvider split — IGNORED (sidesteps: docs use WorkingDir not State)
2. Stable-key cursors — CONTRADICTED. Uses encodeCursor(i) slice-index helper from resolver_node.go, which base64-wraps "cursor:%d". Doc IDs are already sorted lexically — a stable key was trivially available. Debt doubled: slice-index pattern now spans 2 resolver files.
3. Snapshot struct vs direct types — CONTRADICTED (mildly). Uses direct docgraph.Document types (doesnt duplicate as Snapshot). Opposite of resolver_node which uses Snapshot structs. Inconsistent pattern in the codebase.
4. Nil-checks via constructor — CONTRADICTED. Adds "if r.WorkingDir == empty" early-return at two call sites instead of requiring non-empty at construction.
5. Prefix registry for node(id) — NOT APPLICABLE (no node(id) touched).

VERDICT: APPROVE_WITH_EDITS. Do not reopen — acceptance is met, feature works. File a refactor follow-up bead so Stage 2.5+ does not compound the pattern. The slice-index cursor, nil-check, and snapshot-strategy inconsistency are pre-existing from Stage 2.2 that this bead extended rather than introduced.

TESTS: TestGraphQLDocuments (0.00s), TestGraphQLDocGraph (0.00s) both PASS. go build ./... clean.
source: opus review of 45ef7ca (claude sonnet retry after vidar stall)
summary: APPROVE_WITH_EDITS: real resolvers + real tests pass; 4 of 5 shape concerns contradicted — cursor-index pattern now extended to docs, nil-check pattern repeated, snapshot strategy inconsistent with resolver_node. File follow-up refactor bead. Do not reopen.
